mechanism_narrative: Declines in the federal funds rate directly reduce corporate borrowing costs and compress equity discount rates, generating positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next month.
failure_condition: Rank IC falls below 0.02 over a 3-year rolling window or forward returns show no statistical significance (p > 0.10) during rate cuts.

mechanism_narrative: A unemployment rate crossing above 5.0 percent signals deteriorating labor market conditions that trigger immediate risk-off positioning, producing negative S&P 500 returns within one week.
failure_condition: Positive 5-day forward returns occur in more than 55 percent of instances when UNRATE exceeds 5.0 percent.

mechanism_narrative: Simultaneous expansion in real personal consumption expenditures and housing starts indicates synchronized consumer and builder confidence, driving positive equity returns over a quarter.
failure_condition: The joint signal yields a negative or zero information coefficient over any 24-month out-of-sample period.

mechanism_narrative: Rising industrial production indices during periods of stable inflation reflect genuine capacity utilization gains that support corporate earnings and generate positive monthly equity returns.
failure_condition: Forward returns turn negative when CPIAUCSL exceeds 3.0 percent annualized despite rising INDPRO.

mechanism_narrative: Accelerating consumer price inflation above 0.4 percent monthly forces markets to price in aggressive central bank tightening, causing immediate negative equity returns over the following week.
failure_condition: S&P 500 posts positive 5-day returns in over 60 percent of months where CPIAUCSL rises faster than 0.4 percent.

mechanism_narrative: Divergence between rising total nonfarm payrolls and falling unemployment rates confirms robust labor market tightening that sustains consumer spending and drives positive quarterly equity returns.
failure_condition: The combined labor signal produces a negative Sharpe ratio over 60-day forward windows in backtests spanning 2005 to 2017.

mechanism_narrative: Higher quarterly real gross domestic product growth directly increases corporate revenue expectations and equity valuations, generating positive S&P 500 returns over the subsequent month.
failure_condition: Quarterly GDPC1 revisions show no predictive power for 20-day forward returns, with a t-statistic below 1.5 over the full sample.

mechanism_narrative: Declining industrial production during a monetary tightening cycle signals supply-side constraints that outweigh demand, triggering rapid negative equity returns within one week.
failure_condition: Positive 5-day returns dominate when INDPRO falls during periods where FEDFUNDS is above 2.0 percent.
